notable_text: Tonight, at my direction, brave American forces and the Armed Forces of Nigeria flawlessly executed a meticulously planned and very complex mission to eliminate the most active terrorist in the world from the battlefield... GOD BLESS AMERICA! President DONALD J. TRUMP
description: A designed graphic styled as a screenshot of a Truth Social / Twitter-style post from Donald J. Trump (@realDonaldTrump). At the top-left is a circular profile avatar showing Trump's face stylized/painted in the colors and stars-and-stripes pattern of the American flag (blue face with white stars on one side, red-and-white stripes on the other). Next to it is the bold display name 'Donald J. Trump' followed by a pink/magenta verified checkmark badge and a small black square badge (the Truth Social logo). Below the name is the grey handle '@realDonaldTrump'. The body is a long black-on-white statement in a clean sans-serif font reading verbatim: 'Tonight, at my direction, brave American forces and the Armed Forces of Nigeria flawlessly executed a meticulously planned and very complex mission to eliminate the most active terrorist in the world from the battlefield. Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, second in command of ISIS globally, thought he could hide in Africa, but little did he know we had sources who kept us informed on what he was doing. He will no longer terrorize the people of Africa, or help plan operations to target Americans. With his removal, ISIS's global operation is greatly diminished. Thank you to the Government of Nigeria for your partnership on this operation. GOD BLESS AMERICA! President DONALD J. TRUMP'. The visual style is minimalist (white background, black text) but the post itself is a curated, branded social-media artifact rather than a candid photo. The propaganda technique is the projection of decisive military strength and presidential authority ('at my direction'), nationalist framing ('GOD BLESS AMERICA'), and a narrative of America protecting both Americans and Africans from terrorism. Target audience: the administration's social-media following and the general public, signaling toughness on terrorism and counterterror success.

notable_text: People live in homes, not corporations. | I am immediately taking steps to ban large institutional investors from buying more single-family homes
description: A polished portrait-orientation quote card. The upper two-thirds is a color photograph of President Donald Trump mid-speech, leaning toward a microphone with his right hand raised in a gesture, wearing a dark navy suit, white shirt, and red tie with a small American-flag lapel pin. Behind him is a softly blurred White House interior decorated for the holidays: green garland with warm bokeh string-lights, a framed painting on a wood-paneled wall to the left, and a dark blue flag (presidential or US flag) to the right. The lower third is a dark navy semi-transparent overlay carrying white body text, with a faint large quotation-mark glyph and a ghosted circular seal watermark behind the text. The text reads verbatim: 'For a very long time, buying and owning a home was considered the pinnacle of the American Dream. It was the reward for working hard, and doing the right thing, but now, because of the Record High Inflation caused by Joe Biden and the Democrats in Congress, that American Dream is increasingly out of reach for far too many people, especially younger Americans.' followed by a bolded passage: 'It is for that reason, and much more, that I am immediately taking steps to ban large institutional investors from buying more single-family homes, and I will be calling on Congress to codify it. People live in homes, not corporations.' Attribution at bottom-left in bold: 'President Donald J. Trump' and in italics 'Truth Social, January 7th, 2026'. The composition is the standard White House messaging-card template (photo + dark gradient + pull-quote). Propaganda technique: populist economic framing (the 'American Dream' threatened by inflation blamed on Biden/Democrats), positioning Trump as the protector of ordinary/young Americans against corporate landlords with the memorable slogan 'People live in homes, not corporations.' Target audience: working- and middle-class Americans, especially younger would-be homebuyers.

The line starts near 3,015,000 in January 2025 and declines steadily, then drops sharply between September and October 2025 to about 2,750,000, flattening near 2,745,000 by November. The simple typography and lone descending line are designed to dramatize a steep cut in the federal workforce. Per the tweet, it claims federal employment is 'now at the lowest level since 2014 — down by 271,000 jobs since President Trump took office. Promises made, promises kept.' The accompanying tweet frames this as a call to put 'AMERICA FIRST,' end offshoring and the prioritization of foreign labor. Target audience: working-class Americans, manufacturing/blue-collar workers and economic-nationalist supporters of the administration's protectionist agenda.

description: A slogan graphic from the U.S. Department of Labor that overlays text onto a famous fine-art history painting. The artwork is Howard Pyle's 'The Nation Makers,' depicting a column of Revolutionary War Continental soldiers and militia advancing across a field of battle: ragged patriots in white shirts and tricorn hats march forward carrying muskets with fixed bayonets and shouldered rifles, one beating a red field drum, with a tattered American flag (early stars-and-stripes) and a uniformed officer leading at the left, painted in a warm, heroic, painterly style under a smoke-streaked sky. Across the bottom of the image a small DOL/eagle emblem sits above large white serif capital text reading 'PATRIOTISM, NOT GLOBALISM' set against a subtle dark gradient bar. Propaganda technique: appropriates revered Revolutionary-era patriotic imagery to lend historical and moral weight to the administration's economic-nationalist message, framing 'patriotism' and 'globalism' as opposites. The tweet ('2026 is the year of the Patriot. AMERICA FIRST. AMERICANS FIRST.') reinforces the nationalist framing and ties to the 250th-anniversary theme. Target audience: economic nationalists, blue-collar/American-worker base, patriotic supporters.

At top is the Chicago Tribune masthead in its signature blue blackletter (Old English) font, with a thin rule beneath it and a breadcrumb 'NEWS > CRIME AND PUBLIC SAFETY' in gray sans-serif. Below, in the Tribune's black serif headline font, is the original headline 'Chicago police arrest [Rogers Park man] in Loyola slaying' — but DHS has editorially altered it: the words 'Rogers Park man' are struck through with a hand-drawn red line, and red italic bold capitalized text 'TWICE RELEASED CRIMINAL ILLEGAL ALIEN' has been inserted above/over that line. The effect reads: 'Chicago police arrest TWICE RELEASED CRIMINAL ILLEGAL ALIEN [~~Rogers Park man~~] in Loyola slaying.' The propaganda technique is a 'fixed the headline for you' edit attacking a mainstream newspaper for an alleged euphemistic 'omission' (calling the suspect a 'Rogers Park man' rather than an illegal alien), reinforcing the immigrant-crime narrative. Companion tweet text accuses the Tribune of a 'BLATANT OMISSION' regarding the murder of Sheridan Gorman. Target audience: supporters receptive to media-bias and immigrant-crime framing.

The top header band is blue with bold white text: 'MESSAGE TO ILLEGAL ALIENS:' (smaller line) over 'A WARNING TO SELF-DEPORT' (large). The upper/blue section, headed 'Benefits:', shows a photograph of a hand holding a smartphone displaying the CBP Home app screen (titled 'Departing Traveler' with a map of the U.S. and menu items 'Verify Departure', 'View My Departure', 'Submit Intent To Depart'). The listed benefits in white text: 'Self-deportation is safe. Leave on your own terms by picking your departure flight.'; 'Keep money earned in the U.S. If you self-deport as a non-criminal illegal alien.'; 'Future opportunity for legal immigration. The circular blue DHS seal appears bottom-right. The propaganda technique is a classic carrot-and-stick / fear appeal: blue 'safe' benefits versus red 'threat' consequences, pressuring undocumented immigrants to leave voluntarily. Target audience: undocumented immigrants in the U.S.

notable_text: Green cards and visas are a privilege, not a right.
description: A clean USCIS designed slogan graphic on a pale blue gradient background watermarked with a faint, large 'DEPARTMENT OF ... HOMELAND ...' seal/wreath behind the text. The headline, set in a heavy dark-navy serif font at upper left, reads: 'Green cards and visas are a privilege, not a right.' with the word 'privilege' rendered in bold italic and underlined with a hand-drawn red swoosh for emphasis. Below the headline are two overlapping specimen identity documents shown as photographs: a U.S. 'PERMANENT RESIDENT' green card (sample bearing name 'TEST V SPECIMEN', USCIS# 000-000-310, Category IR1, Country of Birth Bahamas, Date of Birth 17 AUG 1958, with a Statue of Liberty/flag background and a sample headshot of a man) and a U.S. 'IMMIGRANT VISA' (Issuing Post US Consulate General London, surname TRAVELER, given name HAPPYPERSON, Category IR1, with a sample headshot of a woman and a machine-readable zone reading 'VIUSATRAVELER<<HAPPYPERSON<<...'). The propaganda/messaging technique is a stern legal-warning framing that immigration status is revocable and conditional, supporting the tweet's point that 'rigorous security vetting does not end' and lawbreakers will 'lose your privileges.' Target audience: visa and green-card holders / immigrants, signaling enforcement and the threat of revocation.

genre_refs: testimonial pull-quote card; American diner Americana
notable_text: "I Got Back Every Penny" Nicole Mendoza, 68-year-old waitress, on filing her taxes thanks to No Tax on Overtime. New York Times
description: A designed White House testimonial/quote card built on a darkened photograph of an American diner: a large American flag hangs on the wall, a waitress in an apron (a '...0 Diner' shirt partially visible) stands at left, and a customer with red-tinted lighting sits in a checkerboard-floored booth. The photo is heavily dimmed and red/dark-toned so overlaid text is legible. Centered near the top is the engraved White House line-drawing emblem. Below it, in large white serif with curly quotation marks, is the pull-quote 'I Got Back Every Penny'. A white sans-serif attribution follows: 'Nicole Mendoza, 68-year-old waitress, on filing her taxes thanks to' and then a red highlight box reading 'No Tax on Overtime'. Lower center, in bold white, is the source 'New York Times'. The propaganda technique is a relatable everyday-citizen testimonial paired with mainstream-media sourcing to humanize and validate the administration's 'Working Families Tax Cuts' / No Tax on Overtime, Tips, and Social Security policy. Target audience: working- and middle-class taxpayers, especially tipped/hourly service workers and seniors.

notable_text: GE VERNOVA FACTORY LEADER HIGHLIGHTS U.S. JOB CREATION FROM FOREIGN INVESTMENT DURING TRUMP-MBS MEETING; over $750 million in the U.S. focused on true manufacturing jobs here stateside
description: A designed share-card styled as a Breitbart News article. At the top is a black bar with the white Breitbart 'B' logo and 'BREITBART' wordmark. Below, on a white field, is a large bold black condensed headline with key phrases highlighted in yellow: 'GE VERNOVA FACTORY LEADER HIGHLIGHTS U.S. JOB CREATION FROM FOREIGN INVESTMENT DURING TRUMP-MBS MEETING'. A pull-quote in serif text reads: 'If you look at the landscape for GE Vernova investment, over $750 million in the U.S. focused on true manufacturing jobs here stateside,' he said. 'We're looking at tripling the output of our Greenville, South Carolina, facility, where we make the gas turbines that are supporting U.S. needs as well as the Saudi Arabian needs.' The lower two-thirds is a photograph of President Trump (left, dark navy suit, red tie, gesturing) speaking with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman (right, in white thobe and red-and-white checkered keffiyeh with gold-trimmed bisht) in an ornate gilded White House room; press photographers are visible in the background and a framed portrait hangs on the wall. The composition imitates a credible news clipping to lend third-party authority. The messaging frames foreign (Saudi) investment as creating American manufacturing jobs, supporting the 'America First / more opportunities for American workers' narrative. Target audience is the administration's supporters and the general public consuming pro-administration economic news.

genre_refs: Art Deco / WPA propaganda poster style; Lincoln Memorial
notable_text: YOUR NATION CALLS; APPRENTICESHIP.GOV
description: A stylized Art Deco recruitment/propaganda poster rendered as a flat vector illustration in a sepia, gold, cream and dark-navy palette. The central figure is a heroic, monumentalized depiction of the seated Abraham Lincoln statue from the Lincoln Memorial, with Lincoln seated in his throne-like chair flanked by the two fasces-topped columns of the memorial's armrests. Behind him radiate dramatic geometric sunburst rays and stepped Art Deco/skyscraper motifs, with a glowing halo behind his head, giving an almost religious, idealized aura. Across the bottom in bold cream all-caps sans-serif is the slogan 'YOUR NATION CALLS', and beneath it in smaller spaced caps the URL 'APPRENTICESHIP.GOV'. The retro 1930s WPA-poster aesthetic evokes patriotic civic duty and industrial mobilization. Per the tweet ('ANSWER THE CALL'), this is part of the DOL 'Make America Skilled Again' apprenticeship recruitment campaign, urging Americans to enter the skilled trades. Target audience is young and working-age Americans considering apprenticeships/skilled careers, framed as a patriotic national-service calling.

notable_text: PROTECTING AMERICANS; The Charlotte Observer; nearly 200 investigations ... abusing the H-1B visa program as part of Project Firewall
description: A dark navy-bordered Department of Labor share-card in portrait format. The top two-thirds is a photograph of President Trump standing at a lectern in the U.S. Capitol (House chamber) wearing a navy suit and maroon/purple tie with an American flag lapel pin; behind him Vice President JD Vance (left) is clapping and Speaker Mike Johnson (center) stands, with American flags and ornate columns visible. Centered in large white serif all-caps is the headline 'PROTECTING AMERICANS'. Below, on a white rounded panel, is the masthead of 'The Charlotte Observer' (in its classic blue blackletter logo) lending the appearance of a news report, followed by serif body text: 'The Trump Administration is taking decisive action to ensure American job opportunities are not being replaced by cheap foreign labor.' and 'The Labor Department launched nearly 200 investigations into companies suspected of abusing the H-1B visa program as part of Project Firewall, an effort to ensure employers do not unlawfully displace American workers.' The DOL seal appears at the very bottom. The technique borrows a newspaper masthead to add third-party credibility to the administration's H-1B enforcement messaging, framing immigration/visa enforcement as protecting American jobs. Target audience is the public and DOL followers supportive of restricting foreign labor.

notable_text: Recapture the Dream.
description: A nostalgic, warm-toned propaganda graphic in portrait format. The photograph shows a classic two-story American suburban home (a gray Victorian/Folk-Victorian house with a covered front porch, decorative gables, and surrounding green trees) at golden hour; the porch and windows glow with warm light, an American flag hangs from a porch column, a mailbox stands at the curb, and the image carries a heavy sepia/amber vintage filter evoking 1970s film photography and Americana. Centered over the house in a white serif font (with a subtle glow) is the slogan 'Recapture the Dream.' A small white Department of Labor seal sits just above the text. The aesthetic deliberately evokes idealized small-town American homeownership and 'the American Dream.' Per the tweet, the message credits President Trump with 'recapturing the American Dream,' reversing 'Biden-era wage declines,' helping Americans afford homes, and ensuring 'American Jobs go to American Workers.' The propaganda technique relies on warm nostalgia and aspirational homeownership imagery rather than data. Target audience is working- and middle-class Americans, tying immigration restriction and labor policy to the promise of affordable homeownership.

notable_text: Blue Collar BOOM!; Real Blue Collar Wage Boom; Trump (2025) 1.4
description: A Department of Labor share-card in portrait format with a dark navy gradient frame. The upper half is a photograph of a bearded young blue-collar factory worker wearing a backwards dark ball cap, clear safety glasses, and a navy work coverall with an 'EXCEL' patch and a 'Zack' name badge, standing in an industrial machine shop full of green/teal equipment, hoses, gauges and pipes. Centered over the lower portion of the photo is the slogan in white type: 'Blue Collar' in italic serif and 'BOOM!' in bold serif. The bottom half is a white rounded chart card titled 'Real Blue Collar Wage Boom' with subtitle 'Year-to-Date, Seasonally Adjusted Annual Rate (%)' and a U.S. Treasury Department seal in the corner. The bar chart compares values by president: Nixon (1969) 0.2, Carter (1977) 0.2, Reagan (1981) -2.1, H.W. Bush (1989) -1.5, Clinton (1993) 0.0, W. Bush (2001) 0.4, Obama (2009) -1.5, Trump (2017) 1.5, Biden (2021) -1.7, Trump (2025) 1.4; sourced to 'Bureau of Labor Statistics.' The DOL seal appears at the bottom. The technique pairs an authentic-looking working-class hero image with a partisan comparative chart to claim blue-collar wages are rising fastest under Trump ('Promises made, promises kept'). Target audience is blue-collar and working-class voters.

genre_refs: WWII-era industrial mobilization propaganda poster; Statue of Liberty
notable_text: YOUR NATION IS CALLING!
description: A retro illustrated recruitment/propaganda poster styled after WWII-era American industrial-mobilization art, in warm painterly tones of gold, cream, blue and rust. The dominant figure is the Statue of Liberty rising from billowing clouds, holding her torch aloft with brilliant golden sunrays bursting from the flame across the sky. In the lower portion is a busy industrial scene: a ship under construction with a dockside crane, smokestacks and steel-mill/blast-furnace structures emitting smoke, and aircraft, evoking shipbuilding and heavy manufacturing. Across the right-center in bold white serif all-caps is the slogan 'YOUR NATION IS CALLING!' A small white Department of Labor seal sits in the bottom-left corner. The aesthetic deliberately mimics mid-century patriotic call-to-work posters. Per the tweet, this is part of the 'Make America Skilled Again' campaign ('From shipbuilding to AI, the future of America will be built by our Skilled Workers'), recruiting Americans into skilled trades. Target audience is working-age Americans, framing industrial/skilled work as a patriotic national duty.

notable_text: Trust TRUMP! | FOX BUSINESS: Trump administration prioritizes bolstering skilled trades workforce | The Washington Times: Trump's aluminum and steel tariffs are working | Bloomberg: No More Offshore. Startups Look to Spend and Hire in U.S. Due to Trump Tax Change. | THE WALL STREET JOURNAL: Companies Tout More Investments, Orders From Trump's Tax Bill in Earnings Calls
description: A designed Department of Labor propaganda graphic. The left half is a full-height color photograph of President Donald Trump striding forward in a dark navy suit, white shirt, red tie, and a small American flag lapel pin, against a blurred background of grass and the side of a presidential helicopter (Marine One) at the White House. A large white serif headline at the top reads 'Trust TRUMP!' ('Trust' in italic, 'TRUMP!' in bold capitals). The right half stacks four faux torn-newspaper clippings, each with a real news outlet logo and a positive headline about the administration's economic record: FOX BUSINESS - 'Trump administration prioritizes bolstering skilled trades workforce'; The Washington Times - 'Trump's aluminum and steel tariffs are working'; Bloomberg - 'No More Offshore. Startups Look to Spend and Hire in U.S. Due to Trump Tax Change.'; THE WALL STREET JOURNAL - 'Companies Tout More Investments, Orders From Trump's Tax Bill in Earnings Calls'. A small embossed seal sits at the bottom center. The visual style is a clean, high-contrast political-poster montage using the torn-paper headline-collage technique to borrow credibility from mainstream outlets. The propaganda technique is testimonial/authority-by-association (citing third-party press) combined with a personality-cult slogan ('Trust TRUMP!'). The target audience is the general public and pro-administration social-media followers, intended to project economic success and reinforce loyalty to the President.

notable_text: SECOND TO NONE.
description: A cinematic, landscape-format patriotic propaganda graphic with a vintage-film color grade (teal sky, warm skin tones, light grain and lens artifacts). The image is shot from a low angle behind President Trump, seen from the back/side with his distinctive blond hair, raising his right hand in a salute toward the sky. To the left, a large American flag waves on a pole. Overhead in the teal sky, two military aircraft fly: a fighter jet (upper center) and a B-2 stealth bomber (upper right), evoking a military flyover. In glowing white stylized/condensed caps to the right is the slogan 'SECOND TO NONE.' The composition is heroic and reverent, glorifying American military strength and Trump as commander-in-chief. Per the tweet, the message claims Trump is the first president in decades to ensure all net job growth goes to American-born workers ('AMERICANS FIRST'), though the image itself foregrounds military might/patriotism. Target audience is patriotic Americans and the administration's base, fusing nationalism and military pride with the America First labor message.

notable_text: Donald J. Trump @realDonaldTrump | Blame the Democrats for the Airport's mess... They broke the already signed Bill because they want to take care of Criminals who enter our Country illegally, rather than American citizens. Thank you to our great ICE Patriots for helping... I may call up the National Guard for more help. President DJT.
description: A designed graphic styled as a clean screenshot of a Donald Trump social-media post (Truth Social / X style), shared by DHS. At the top is a circular profile avatar showing a stylized portrait of Trump's face overlaid with the American flag (stars-and-stripes face graphic), the display name 'Donald J. Trump' with a magenta/pink verified checkmark badge and a small dark verification/boost icon, and the handle '@realDonaldTrump'. Below, in large black sans-serif text on a white background, is the full quoted post: 'Blame the Democrats for the Airport's mess. They want our Country to do badly. They want our Country to fail. They broke the already signed Bill because they want to take care of Criminals who enter our Country illegally, rather than American citizens. Thank you to our great ICE Patriots for helping. It makes a big difference. I may call up the National Guard for more help. President DJT.' The visual style is a minimalist quote-card screenshot. The propaganda technique is partisan blame-shifting (attributing airport/TSA shutdown chaos to Democrats), othering of unauthorized immigrants as 'Criminals,' and praise of ICE as 'Patriots.' The target audience is the general public and supporters, intended to assign blame for a government shutdown's effects and to valorize immigration enforcement.

notable_text: Calling ALL Attorneys; Join us at our virtual job fair. December 11; ICE.gov/careers
description: A polished designed recruitment graphic for an ICE attorney job fair. The right two-thirds shows a warm, golden-lit photographic/illustrated rendering of a bronze Lady Justice (Themis) statue, blindfolded, holding aloft a set of balance scales in her raised right hand; sunlight streams through what appears to be an arched window behind her. On the left, a translucent, rippling American flag is overlaid, fading into the bright upper-left corner. Bold dark-navy serif text in the upper left reads 'Calling ALL Attorneys' (with 'ALL' enlarged). The lower third is a solid dark teal/navy band containing white serif text: 'Join us at our virtual job fair.' and 'December 11' with smaller text '11 a.m. - 1 p.m. or 5 p.m. - 7 p.m.' At bottom left is the DHS seal beside the 'ICE | Office of the Principal Legal Advisor' logo, and at bottom right 'ICE.gov/careers'. The composition uses patriotic and judicial symbolism (Lady Justice, the flag, warm 'golden' lighting) to lend gravitas and prestige to a government legal recruitment pitch. Target audience: licensed attorneys being recruited into ICE's Office of the Principal Legal Advisor (OPLA) to prosecute immigration cases.

notable_text: FOX NEWS | Labor Dept launches multimillion-dollar shipbuilding training plan as US races to close China gap
description: A designed Department of Labor 'news-headline card' graphic. The image is a photograph of a U.S. Navy amphibious assault ship (an LHD-type vessel with hull number 6 visible on the flight deck) under way at sea, viewed head-on from astern, with several aircraft and tilt-rotor V-22 Osprey craft parked on the flight deck and an American flag flying from the superstructure; a second warship follows behind, with a hazy mountainous coastline on the horizon. Overlaid across the top is the FOX NEWS logo and a large bold black serif headline: 'Labor Dept launches multimillion-dollar shipbuilding training plan as US races to close China gap.' A small embossed agency/White House seal appears in the lower-left corner. The visual style is a clean screenshot-style news card pairing a militarized, patriotic naval image with a real outlet's headline. The propaganda technique is authority-by-association (citing Fox News) and nationalist framing of workforce policy as great-power competition with China. The target audience is the general public and the skilled-trades/maritime workforce, intended to portray the administration as strengthening American industrial and military might.

notable_text: AMERICA FIRST | SEAL OF THE PRESIDENT OF THE UNITED STATES (jacket emblem)
description: A designed Department of Labor propaganda poster. It is a dramatic high-contrast black-and-white photograph of President Donald Trump rendering a military-style hand salute, his face turned slightly upward with a solemn expression. He wears a dark zip jacket bearing the embroidered circular 'SEAL OF THE PRESIDENT OF THE UNITED STATES' emblem on the left chest, over a shirt and tie. Behind him, out of focus, are an aircraft (Air Force One / a jet) and stadium-style signage with large numerals. A large bold all-caps white serif headline across the top reads 'AMERICA FIRST'. The visual style is a heroic, monochrome, reverent portrait emphasizing patriotism, authority, and military bearing. The propaganda technique is leader glorification / personality-cult imagery combined with the nationalist 'America First' slogan. The target audience is the general public and supporters, intended to project strong, dignified, patriotic leadership and reinforce the America First brand around labor/jobs messaging.

genre_refs: Rembrandt Peale, 'Washington before Yorktown' (classical history painting)
notable_text: One American Heritage
description: A designed Department of Labor patriotic/heritage graphic built on a classical history painting. The base image is Rembrandt Peale's equestrian portrait 'Washington before Yorktown,' depicting General George Washington in his blue-and-buff Continental Army uniform with gold epaulettes mounted on a rearing pale/white horse, with an aide on horseback doffing his hat beside him, other mounted officers and soldiers in tricorn hats in the wooded background, all in the warm oil-painting palette of late-18th-century academic art. Overlaid across the bottom in elegant white cursive script is the slogan 'One American Heritage'. The visual style co-opts fine-art Revolutionary-era imagery to convey gravitas and continuity. The propaganda technique is appeal to founding-era patriotism, national heritage, and unity (paired in the tweet with a George Washington quote about the 'sacred fire of liberty' and the nation's 250th anniversary). The target audience is the general public, intended to wrap the administration's messaging in reverent national-founding symbolism.

notable_text: Since the establishment of the MEMPHIS SAFE TASK FORCE: 7,416 Arrests; 1,219 Illegal Firearms Seized; 150 Missing Children Located; 772 Known Gang Members Arrested
description: A designed White House statistics/infographic card. The background is a heavily darkened nighttime photo of Memphis's Beale Street entertainment district, with neon signage faintly visible (including a Hard Rock Cafe guitar sign) and a street receding into the distance, all dimmed nearly to black for legibility. White typography is layered on top: an elegant serif intro line 'Since the establishment of the' followed by a large bold all-caps serif title 'MEMPHIS SAFE TASK FORCE:', a thin white horizontal divider rule, and then a vertical list of bold sans-serif statistics: '7,416 Arrests', '1,219 Illegal Firearms Seized', '150 Missing Children Located', '772 Known Gang Members Arrested'. A small white White House seal sits centered at the bottom. The visual style is a clean, somber dark-mode stat card. The propaganda technique is quantified law-and-order messaging, using big enforcement numbers (especially the emotionally resonant 'missing children' figure) to project crime-fighting success of a federal task force in a Democratic-led city. The target audience is the general public, intended to promote the administration's urban crime-crackdown initiatives.

notable_text: The Biden JOB MARKET | Change in Employment Level August 2023-2024 | NATIVE-BORN WORKERS: -1,319,000 | FOREIGN-BORN WORKERS: +1,240,000
description: A U.S. Department of Labor designed infographic on a dark navy/black background. The top portion is a photograph (tinted blue to match the theme) of the exterior of the 'Frances Perkins Department of Labor Building', with the carved building name visible on the stone facade. Across the middle in large white serif type (italic 'The Biden' + bold 'JOB MARKET') is the headline 'The Biden JOB MARKET'. Below sits a white rounded-rectangle data card titled in italics 'Change in Employment Level August 2023-2024', containing an embedded FRED (Federal Reserve Economic Data) bar chart comparing monthly 'Employment Level - Native Born' (dark blue bars) versus 'Employment Level - Foreign Born' (light blue bars) from Aug 2023 to Aug 2024, sourced 'U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics via FRED.' Beneath the chart, two large summary lines read 'NATIVE-BORN WORKERS: -1,319,000' and 'FOREIGN-BORN WORKERS: +1,240,000', with a fine-print source line 'Sources: BLS, Federal Reserve Bank, Fox News.' The technique uses official-looking statistics and government/Fed branding to argue that the prior (Biden) administration favored foreign-born over native-born workers. Target audience: American workers and the administration's base, providing a 'data-backed' contrast to justify the current 'Americans First' labor agenda.

notable_text: Skilled Trades BOOM! | High school students turn to trade schools amid rising college costs | More North Texas students are turning to trade schools for faster, affordable career paths | Graduation season brings increased interest in trade schools | From debt to dollars: Gen Z shifts from college loans to skilled trades
description: A designed graphic from the U.S. Department of Labor on a dark navy-blue background that is faintly watermarked with the DOL/government seal. At the top, in a large white serif/italic-and-bold headline, reads 'Skilled Trades BOOM!'. Below the headline are four stacked, white, torn-paper-style clippings, each topped with a recognizable news outlet logo bar and presenting a real (or real-styled) news headline in bold black serif type. Top to bottom they read: '3 NEWS' (a local affiliate) - 'High school students turn to trade schools amid rising college costs'; 'CBS NEWS' (black bar with the CBS eye) - 'More North Texas students are turning to trade schools for faster, affordable career paths: Accepted'; '41 KSHB' (NBC affiliate) - 'Graduation season brings increased interest in trade schools'; and 'The Salt Lake Tribune' - 'From debt to dollars: Gen Z shifts from college loans to skilled trades'. The composition mimics a press-clipping montage to lend third-party credibility to the administration's pro-trade-school / anti-college-debt messaging. The propaganda technique is the appeal to authority and 'bandwagon' framing - assembling multiple mainstream outlets to suggest a broad, validated trend. Target audience: young people (Gen Z), parents, and skilled-trade workers, promoting trade careers as an alternative to expensive college, aligned with the administration's apprenticeship and 'American workers' agenda.

notable_text: Recapture the Dream
description: A designed Department of Labor nostalgia-themed slogan graphic. The image is a warm, faded, vintage-toned (sepia/retro color-graded) photograph of an idyllic mid-20th-century American scene: a classic 1956 Chevrolet Bel Air two-tone turquoise-and-white car parked in the driveway of a tidy two-story brick suburban house with a manicured green lawn, shade trees, and power lines overhead, evoking a 1950s 'American Dream' postwar suburb. Overlaid in the center is a small white DOL eagle emblem and a large white flowing script slogan 'Recapture the Dream'. The visual style is deliberate retro/nostalgia aesthetic with elegant cursive typography. The propaganda technique is appeal to nostalgia, evoking an idealized prosperous past to imply the administration is restoring lost middle-class prosperity and homeownership. The target audience is the general public and American workers, framing the administration's economic agenda as a return to a golden era.

notable_text: Americans FIRST! | Daily Mail: Project Firewall, the DOL's new H1B visa program, already has 200 active investigations into US companies with potential violations. Violations range from misleading applicants about pay to offering jobs solely to non-citizens...
description: A designed Department of Labor propaganda graphic. The upper portion is a color photograph of President Donald Trump in a dark navy suit, light blue shirt, and blue tie with a small American flag lapel pin, standing facing the camera with a slight smirk in front of a blurred dark-blue backdrop showing a partial '202_' year numeral and an out-of-focus map graphic (an event stage). Beneath, a large serif headline reads 'Americans FIRST!' ('Americans' in italic, 'FIRST!' in bold capitals). Below sits a white rounded text card topped with the Daily Mail logo, quoting: 'Project Firewall, the DOL's new H1B visa program, already has 200 active investigations into US companies with potential violations. Violations range from misleading applicants about pay to offering jobs solely to non-citizens. Another issue arises when foreign workers overstay their visas, contributing to the US's immigration woes.' A small embossed seal appears at the bottom center. The visual style is a clean political-poster montage with a press quote-card. The propaganda technique is nationalist sloganeering plus authority-by-association (Daily Mail) to promote the H-1B-visa crackdown 'Project Firewall,' framing visa enforcement and 'foreign workers' as threats to American jobs. The target audience is the general public and American workers.

notable_text: America FIRST! | Bloomberg: No president has ever launched a program quite like Firewall... 'the first time the federal government has sought to broadly enforce H-1B legal standards'... Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer, who will lead the project, said she will personally certify investigations...
description: A designed Department of Labor propaganda graphic. The top portion is a darkened photograph of President Donald Trump in a dark navy suit and red tie raising a clenched fist, standing in front of a blurred dark-green background with Marine One faintly visible at left. Beneath, a large white serif headline reads 'America FIRST!' ('America' in italic, 'FIRST!' in bold capitals). Below sits a white rounded text card topped by the Bloomberg logo, quoting a Bloomberg article about Project Firewall: 'No president has ever launched a program quite like Firewall, which the Labor Department said could be "the first time the federal government has sought to broadly enforce H-1B legal standards" in the program's 35-year history. Until now, suspected non-compliance has been largely based on worker complaints, investigated on a case-by-case basis. Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer, who will lead the project, said she will personally certify investigations, which will draw on data and resources from multiple agencies, including the Justice Department, US Citizenship and Immigration Services and others.' The visual style is a heroic political-poster montage with a defiant fist-raise pose plus an authoritative press quote-card. The propaganda technique is nationalist sloganeering ('America FIRST!') combined with authority-by-association (Bloomberg) to promote the H-1B-visa crackdown 'Project Firewall.' The target audience is the general public and American workers, framing visa enforcement as protecting American jobs.

genre_refs: WANTED / BUSTED rogues-gallery card; America's Most Wanted aesthetic
notable_text: ARRESTED BY ICE KNOXVILLE ON FEB. 13, 2025. AGGRAVATED FELON AND RE-ENTRY. WH.GOV
description: A designed White House 'rogues-gallery' arrest card on a high-contrast orange-and-black background (orange evoking jail jumpsuits / a fiery mugshot aesthetic). A black header bar at top carries the word 'ARRESTED' in huge bold orange capitals, with a sub-line in white/orange: 'BY ICE KNOXVILLE ON FEB. 13, 2025'. The center features a real booking mugshot photograph of a dark-haired man (identified by the tweet as Horacio Mejia-Villegas, a Guatemalan national) framed in a thick black border against a detention-facility wall. Below, on black highlight bars, bold orange/cream capitals read 'AGGRAVATED FELON AND RE-ENTRY'. At the bottom center is the engraved White House line-drawing emblem above 'WH.GOV'. The propaganda technique is a fear/criminal-spotlight format that pairs a mugshot with criminal labels to portray immigration enforcement as removing dangerous criminals (the tweet lists 'Rape of a child, assault with a weapon, hit and run, domestic violence...' and 'MAKE AMERICA SAFE AGAIN'). Target audience: the general public and the administration's base, intended to build support for aggressive ICE deportations. Note: zero retweets recorded despite the like count.

notable_text: LABOR DAY 2025 | Blue Collar BOOM! | Real Blue Collar Wage Boom | Trump (2025) 1.4 | Biden (2021) -1.7
description: A U.S. Department of Labor designed infographic on a dark navy background. The upper half is a photograph of a bearded male construction worker wearing a white hard hat, safety glasses, and a red t-shirt, working at an industrial/job site (blurred blue background). Overlaid is a small light-blue banner reading 'LABOR DAY 2025' above a large white serif headline 'Blue Collar BOOM!' (italic 'Blue Collar' + bold 'BOOM!'). Below is a white rounded-rectangle data card titled in bold 'Real Blue Collar Wage Boom' with subtitle 'Year-to-Date, Seasonally Adjusted Annual Rate (%)' and the U.S. Department of the Treasury seal in the top-right corner. The card holds a bar chart comparing real blue-collar wage growth by president at the start of their term: Nixon (1969) 0.2, Carter (1977) 0.2, Reagan (1981) -2.1, H.W. Bush (1989) -1.5, Clinton (1993) 0.0, W. Bush (2001) 0.4, Obama (2009) -1.5, Trump (2017) 1.5, Biden (2021) -1.7, Trump (2025) 1.4; sourced 'Bureau of Labor Statistics.' A small DOL seal sits centered at the bottom. The technique uses official Treasury/BLS branding and a favorable historical comparison to claim record wage growth under Trump. Target audience: blue-collar workers and the administration's base, released for Labor Day to credit the president with the fastest blue-collar wage gains in decades.

notable_text: Protecting AMERICAN JOBS | Newsweek: Project Firewall marks a turning point in how the United States polices high-skilled immigration... the first time in more than three decades that a U.S. labor secretary has pledged to personally certify investigations into suspected H-1B and Labor Condition Application (LCA) violations.
description: A U.S. Department of Labor designed graphic on a near-black background. The upper portion shows a photograph of President Donald Trump seated at the Resolute Desk in the Oval Office, head bowed as he signs a document; behind him are the gold drapes, military service flags (with eagle finials), and a framed historical illustration on the wall. Overlaid in white serif type (italic 'Protecting' above bold 'AMERICAN JOBS') is the slogan 'Protecting AMERICAN JOBS'. notable_text: Over 5,300 criminal aliens arrested throughout L.A., including his district.; U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement

Overlaid on the right side in large type is the statistic: the word 'Over' in gold italic, then '5,300' in very large bold gold numerals, followed by 'criminal aliens arrested throughout L.A., including his district.' in white serif caps/mixed case. genre_refs: FBI Ten Most Wanted Fugitive poster format
notable_text: FBI TEN MOST WANTED FUGITIVE; EDUARDO RAVELO; REWARD up to $100,000
description: A formal FBI 'TEN MOST WANTED FUGITIVE' poster reproduced as a social-media graphic. The layout uses the official FBI template: a white background with a deep-blue masthead bar reading 'FBI TEN MOST WANTED FUGITIVE' in bold serif/condensed caps, with the gold-and-blue FBI / Department of Justice seal at top left. Below the masthead, the subject's name 'EDUARDO RAVELO' appears in very large blue caps. A dense paragraph of blue text lists the federal charges: 'Conspiracy to Conduct the Affairs of an Enterprise, Through a Pattern of Racketeering Activity; Conspiracy to Distribute and Possess with the Intent to Distribute Controlled Substances; Conspiracy to Import Controlled Substances; Conspiracy to Launder Monetary Instruments; Conspiracy to Kill Persons in a Foreign Country; Murder Resulting from the Use and Carrying of a Firearm During and in Relation to Crimes of Violence and Drug Trafficking; Murder in Aid of Racketeering Activity.' Beneath the charges are four images in a row: a 1998 mugshot photograph of a Hispanic man with a goatee, a pencil 'Composite Sketch,' and two 1992 photographs showing tattoos on the man's chest/abdomen and back (each captioned with the year taken). A 'DESCRIPTION' header introduces a two-column biographical block listing Aliases ('Tablas,' 'T-Blas,' '2x4,' 'Blas,' 'Lumberman,' 'Boards,' 'Tablero,' '56,' etc.), Dates of Birth Used, Place of Birth (Mexico), Hair, Eyes, Height (5'9"), Weight, Build, Complexion, Sex, Race ('White (Hispanic)'), Nationality (Mexican), and Scars and Marks. A red 'REWARD' bar states the FBI is offering up to $100,000 for information leading to his arrest. The style is an authentic-looking law-enforcement document rather than a stylized meme, but it is deployed by ICE as a designed propaganda/wanted graphic touting the prosecution of a Barrio Azteca gang leader. Target audience: the public, framing immigration enforcement as capturing dangerous transnational criminals.

notable_text: The Education President; PRESIDENT TRUMP IS MAKING EDUCATION GREAT!; Awarded a record $500 million for charter schools; Rooted out DEI in America's schools; Prevented $1 billion in student aid fraud; Protected women's sports by enforcing Title IX

The lower two-thirds is deep navy with a faint seal watermark. The large white headline reads 'The Education President', followed by a blue ribbon banner 'PRESIDENT TRUMP IS MAKING EDUCATION GREAT!'. notable_text: INTEL STOCK CUMULATIVE TOTAL RETURN SINCE JAN. 2, 2025; 496.5%; AUG. 22, 2025 THE U.S. GOVERNMENT SECURES A 10% STAKE IN INTEL
description: A horizontal data-visualization infographic on a white background, styled like a financial-news line chart (clean, professional, restrained). notable_text: Trade Schools ARE THE FUTURE!; Gen Z, now dubbed the 'toolbelt generation'

notable_text: Economy; added 115k jobs in April; weekly earnings up a massive 5.6%; U.S. Steel $1.9 billion investment; drug pricing policy will save $529 billion over the next decade
description: A designed bullet-point statistics card with a dark navy/slate background. notable_text: If you self-deport now, you MAY be able to return as an immigrant or visitor someday — the LEGAL way; Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation
description: A polished 'self-deport' promotional graphic built around a warm, emotionally positive stock photograph: two people embracing in a tight hug, apparently in an airport terminal (blurred travelers and concourse lighting in the background). One person wears a denim jacket and a small backpack and has a face mask pulled down; the other wears a light-colored sweater. The photo evokes a happy reunion. Overlaid white text in the lower-left reads 'If you self-deport now, you MAY be able to return as an immigrant or visitor someday —' with 'MAY' emphasized, followed by a large headline 'the LEGAL way' where the word 'LEGAL' is set in bold green to contrast with the white 'the' and 'way'. Across the bottom is a dark blue banner bearing the round DHS/ICE seal and the 'ICE' wordmark on the left, with a vertical divider and the call to action 'Learn more at: ICE.gov/self-deportation' on the right. The propaganda technique pairs an upbeat, family-reunion image with the threat-softening promise of possible legal re-entry to encourage voluntary departure ('self-deportation'). Target audience: undocumented immigrants, urging them to leave the country before being arrested.

notable_text: Federal Employment (chart title); axis labels January 2025 - November 2025, 2,750,000 - 3,050,000
description: A clean, minimalist line-chart infographic on a white background titled in large bold black sans-serif type 'Federal Employment'. The y-axis is labeled with employment figures from 2,750,000 up to 3,050,000 in 50,000 increments; the x-axis lists months from January 2025 through November 2025 (labels angled diagonally). A single thick dark navy-blue line plots the data, beginning just above 3,000,000 in January and sloping steadily downward through the year, then dropping sharply between September 2025 (~2,910,000) and October 2025 (~2,750,000) before leveling off slightly lower in November. The styling is deliberately spare and 'data-journalism' looking, lending an air of objectivity. The propaganda technique is to visually dramatize the steep decline in the federal workforce as a positive achievement, paired with the tweet text celebrating that federal employment is at its lowest level in over a decade and crediting President Trump with 'right-sizing' the government. Target audience: supporters who favor shrinking the federal bureaucracy. No agency seal appears on the chart itself.

notable_text: ECONOMY; Central U.S. Manufacturing Activity Accelerates; Tenth District Manufacturing Survey's composite index climbed to 8 this month from 6 in October; By Ed Frankl, Nov. 20, 2025
description: A screenshot styled as a Wall Street Journal online article, presented as a designed share-card. At top a small blue 'ECONOMY' section kicker sits above the bold black serif headline 'Central U.S. Manufacturing Activity Accelerates'. A deck/subhead in gray reads 'Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City says its Tenth District Manufacturing Survey's composite index climbed to 8 this month from 6 in October'. Below is a byline 'By Ed Frankl' with a blue 'Follow' button, timestamp 'Nov. 20, 2025 12:03 pm ET', and a row of WSJ UI controls (Share, Resize, Listen (1 min), and a three-dot menu). The lower half is a color photograph inside an industrial setting: a man in a maroon T-shirt and gray Nike cap, wearing a work glove, working on a large disassembled engine/transmission assembly suspended by a chain hoist; shelving with parts boxes (a red toolbox, a 'Z-03' bin label) recedes behind him on a factory floor. The image is essentially a credibility-borrowing repost of a mainstream-media business story to support the tweet's 'THE TRUMP EFFECT' manufacturing-growth narrative. Target audience: general public, signaling economic momentum under the administration. No government seal is on the image.

The top bears a large bold black all-caps headline 'JD VANCE: NATIVE-BORN CITIZENS ARE DRIVING GROWTH — ‘JOBS ARE GOING TO AMERICAN WORKERS FOR A CHANGE’'. Beneath the headline is Breitbart's row of social-share buttons: a blue Facebook button showing '64', a black 'X / TWEET' button, a gray 'EMAIL' button, and a blue 'TRUTH' (Truth Social) button. Superimposed across the bottom of the still in huge bold white block letters with a drop shadow is the phrase 'AMERICAN JOBS!'. At top center is the masthead 'THE DAILY WIRE' in the publication's distinctive thin-serif caps with horizontal rules flanking the small word 'THE'. A thin grey divider line runs beneath. notable_text: The Trump Administration is fully committed to reopening the government for the American People. At the Department of Labor, we will continue doing as much as we can to put American Workers First during the Democrat Shutdown.

description: A designed propaganda/slogan graphic on a dark navy background. notable_text: (no headline slogan; cartel insignia stamps on drug bricks; 'BLACK AND WHITE' label on a bundle; mugshot height-scale numbers 60-69)
description: A designed composite 'criminal alien' card combining seized-drug evidence imagery with an inset mugshot. The main background photograph shows a large stash of suspected narcotics: many kilo-sized bricks individually wrapped in clear plastic (some stamped with a circular cartel/brand insignia), stacked in a pyramid on a wooden table/floor against a plain wall, with additional taped bundles and bags of white powder in the foreground (one bundle showing 'BLACK AND WHITE' branding). In the top half, a high-contrast monochrome photograph shows Trump in a dark suit, white shirt and tie with an American-flag lapel pin, mid-gesture pointing emphatically to his left, set inside what appears to be an industrial hangar or factory; presidential flags and a banner reading 'JO...' (Joe / a venue name) are partly visible at right, including the circular Seal of the President of the United States. The bottom half overlays a clean white bar chart titled in bold blue text 'President Trump is reversing Biden's industrial decay.' with the subtitle 'ISM PMI Manufacturing, points above/below the expansion threshold.' The chart runs monthly from Nov-2022 to Mar-2026: long navy-blue negative bars dominate the Biden-era months (mostly between -1 and -4), transitioning to red bars in 2025 that climb back toward and above zero, ending with three tall positive red bars in Jan-Mar 2026 (reaching ~+2.6). Target audience: workers, manufacturing supporters, and the administration's economic-messaging base.

notable_text: APPRENTICESHIP.GOV
description: A stylized, painterly Art Deco-influenced illustration in the manner of WPA-era and early-20th-century industrial posters, used as recruitment imagery for the skilled trades. It depicts a male construction worker in profile, head bowed in focused concentration, wearing a blue hard hat, a gray-blue work jacket and a high-visibility yellow safety vest. He is set against a soaring backdrop of golden, sunburst-like geometric beams and blue marbled panels evoking the gilded interior of an Art Deco skyscraper lobby (reminiscent of Rockefeller Center / Chrysler Building ornamentation), bathing the scene in warm gold and blue light. The illustration style is hand-painted/digital-painterly with no photographic elements. At the very bottom, centered white text reads 'APPRENTICESHIP.GOV', above which sits a small circular DOL/government seal. There is no slogan headline; the message is aspirational and dignifying, romanticizing blue-collar craftsmanship and recasting the tradesman as a heroic builder of a new 'Golden Age' (echoing the tweet's 'GREAT AMERICAN WORKFORCE REVIVAL'). Target audience: prospective apprentices and young people considering the skilled trades over college.

The top photo shows a cheerful group of trade/vocational students and an instructor on a workshop or factory floor: a woman in a green top (left) talking with several young workers wearing colorful hard hats (white, orange numbered '51', a green sticker-covered helmet) and safety glasses, smiling. notable_text: FOX NEWS / SANCTUARY CITIES / ICE arrests Latin Kings member after NYC sanctuary release despite assault charge on first responder / DHS says Bryan David Tasiguano Leon, an Ecuadorian national, was arrested by ICE on March 4 after the NYPD freed him over federal objection / POLICE ICE

The design pairs friendly imagery of young tradespeople with a supportive news headline to promote the administration's apprenticeship/skilled-trades agenda. Target audience: general public, workers, and the base, framing DOL grant programs as restoring American skilled labor.

notable_text: AMERICAN MANUFACTURING IS BACK! / Four Straight Months of Manufacturing Expansion / The WHITE HOUSE
description: A polished, vertically oriented designed propaganda poster from The White House. The top banner reads 'AMERICAN MANUFACTURING' in gold/amber serif capitals over a dark red, slightly distressed stripe, with 'IS BACK!' set huge in white italic serif underneath, evoking a triumphant patriotic headline. The central image is a color photograph shot from behind: President Donald Trump in a navy blue suit (seen from the back, with his distinctive blond hair) stands talking with three industrial workers wearing bright orange flame-resistant coveralls/jackets and white hard hats inside a dimly lit factory or shipyard; the workers smile at him. Overlaid at the bottom is a clean white statistics card titled 'Four Straight Months of Manufacturing Expansion' showing a bar chart of a manufacturing index from Apr. 2023 through Apr. 2026, with earlier bars in blue/navy and the most recent four months spiking upward in red (peaking around 52-53), captioned 'Source: ISM Manufacturing PMI Report.' A small White House wordmark and seal ('The WHITE HOUSE') sit at the very bottom. Visual style: dramatic, cinematic lighting, premium serif typography, red-white-and-blue patriotic palette. Propaganda technique: associating the President personally with blue-collar workers and pairing the imagery with a favorable economic chart to credit his 'America First' agenda for a manufacturing rebound. Target audience: working-class Americans and Trump supporters.

notable_text: Labor News; U.S. Department of Labor Awards $86 Million to 14 states for investment in skills training for critical in-demand, emerging industries; DOL.GOV
description: A vertical designed 'Labor News' announcement card built over a dramatic dark photograph of a welder at work. The upper image shows a person in a green long-sleeve shirt and a dark welding helmet (marked 'Lincoln Electric') bent over a metal workpiece, with a bright bluish-white welding arc flaring at center and sparks/glow illuminating the otherwise shadowy industrial interior. A faint U.S. Department of Labor seal watermark is visible in the lower right. Overlaid on a blue rectangular tab is the italic white label 'Labor News' beside four small vertical bars, and below in bold white serif capitals/text is the headline 'U.S. Department of Labor Awards $86 Million to 14 states for investment in skills training for critical in-demand, emerging industries.' Small white text 'DOL.GOV' sits at the bottom right. The dark, heroic industrial aesthetic and quantified grant figure are used to promote the administration's 'Make America Skilled Again' workforce-funding agenda. Target audience: workers, supporters, and the public.

notable_text: NATIONAL SKILLED TRADES DAY / dol.gov / DEPARTMENT OF LABOR UNITED STATES OF AMERICA
description: A vertically oriented designed commemorative/holiday graphic from the U.S. Department of Labor marking 'National Skilled Trades Day.' The background is a dark, moody color action photograph of a tradesman crouched and using a grinder or cutting tool inside an industrial vessel/tank, wearing a clear face shield and work clothes, with a bright shower of orange-yellow sparks fanning out across the right side of the frame against cool blue-gray metal. At the top center is the white line-art official seal of the Department of Labor ('DEPARTMENT OF LABOR / UNITED STATES OF AMERICA' encircling an eagle and shield emblem). Centered at the bottom in elegant white serif typography, flanked by small decorative dashes, is 'NATIONAL SKILLED TRADES DAY,' with 'NATIONAL' in roman caps and 'SKILLED TRADES DAY' in italic caps, and the URL 'dol.gov' beneath. Visual style: dramatic, heroic, high-contrast photography with refined serif lettering and the agency seal lending official gravitas. Messaging: celebrating and dignifying skilled-trades workers as part of the administration's 'Make America Skilled Again' theme. Target audience: tradespeople, workers, and the general public on a commemorative day.

notable_text: EVERETT MUNICIPAL BUILDING / Initiative 3: Control and access to city property / no federal agent conducting immigration enforcement shall be allowed to access non-public areas within a city-owned facility
description: A designed composite graphic from ICE combining a photo with a quoted policy document. The top half is a color photograph of the modern glass-and-wood 'EVERETT MUNICIPAL BUILDING' (its name in large letters on the facade), with decorative red salmon/fish art panels and snow on the ground at the building's base. The bottom half is styled as a torn white sheet of paper (ragged edges top and bottom) overlaid with excerpted ordinance/policy text in black: it is headed 'Initiative 3: Control and access to city property' and includes numbered provisions — '13. Effective immediately, absent a warrant, or other non-immigration related mutual aid investigation in which the Everett Police have sought federal agency assistance, no federal agent conducting immigration enforcement shall be allowed to access non-public areas within a city-owned facility.'; '14. Effective immediately, city employees observing federal agents conducting immigration enforcement or briefing or staging on city owned property shall immediately notify their department director. Department directors shall notify the Mayor's Office or a member of the IRT as soon as practical.'; and '18. Everett police officers who observe an egregious excessive force encounter involving federal immigration officers are expected to take action when it is safe to do so.' Visual style: documentary photo plus a 'leaked/exposed document' torn-paper motif. Technique: spotlighting and condemning a local 'sanctuary' policy (per the tweet, attributed to Everett, WA Mayor Cassie Franklin) as obstructing ICE and endangering officers. Target audience: the public and ICE supporters, framing local officials as adversaries.

notable_text: Working Family TAX CUT / INCREASES TAKE-HOME PAY BY OVER $10,000 ANNUALLY / ELIMINATES TAXES ON TIPS & OVERTIME / CUTS TAXES BY 15% ON AVERAGE FOR HARDWORKING AMERICANS
description: A designed vertical propaganda/infographic poster (White House style) headlined 'Working Family TAX CUT.' The top portion is a color photograph of a cheering rally crowd; in the foreground a smiling woman holds up a toddler wearing a gray T-shirt reading 'american babe,' and a smiling man in a white hard hat (name 'SHAFFER' visible) stands at right, with a partly visible 'THANK YOU PRESIDENT...' sign — conveying enthusiastic working-class support. A dark blue gradient overlays the lower two-thirds. The title is set in white type ('Working Family' in italic serif, 'TAX CUT' in huge bold serif caps). Below is a checklist with white checkmark icons: 'INCREASES TAKE-HOME PAY BY OVER $10,000 ANNUALLY,' 'CREATES NEARLY ONE MILLION NEW JOBS & PROTECTS 6.1 MILLION FULL-TIME JOBS,' 'ELIMINATES TAXES ON TIPS & OVERTIME,' 'INSTITUTES PERMANENT PAID LEAVE TAX CREDIT TO SUPPORT WORKING FAMILIES,' and 'CUTS TAXES BY 15% ON AVERAGE FOR HARDWORKING AMERICANS.' A small White House seal sits at the bottom. Visual style: patriotic navy/white palette, serif headline, clean bulleted benefits list over an emotive crowd photo. Propaganda technique: bullet-point 'benefits' framing plus warm family imagery to sell the tax-cut policy. Target audience: working families and Trump supporters.

notable_text: JOIN DHS. / FORGE YOUR FUTURE WITH DHS / June 5 – 6, 9 AM – 7 PM ET / Dulles Expo Center, Chantilly, VA / DHS CAREER EXPO / MAKE AN IMPACT.
description: A professionally designed vertical recruitment poster for the Department of Homeland Security 'DHS Career Expo.' The layout splits a navy-blue text panel against angled photographic insets. Top-left small caps read 'JOIN DHS.' The large white bold headline states 'FORGE YOUR FUTURE WITH DHS,' followed by event details set off by red vertical accent bars: 'June 5 – 6 / 9 AM – 7 PM ET' and 'Dulles Expo Center / Chantilly, VA.' The imagery is action-oriented and militaristic: at left, two tactical operators in tan/khaki gear and helmets ('POLICE FEDERAL AGENT' visible on a vest) fast-rope down a thick rope; at bottom right, two uniformed personnel in life vests and helmets board or stand atop a low-profile vessel/semi-submersible at sea with a cutter ship in the background (an interdiction scene). Bottom-left shows the round DHS seal beside the 'DHS CAREER EXPO' wordmark, and a bottom banner reads 'MAKE AN IMPACT.' Visual style: corporate-patriotic, clean sans-serif type, navy/red/white palette, heroic operational photography. Technique: glamorizing border-security/tactical careers to recruit applicants for immigration and national-security roles. Target audience: prospective DHS job seekers.

notable_text: A Failing BUREAUCRACY / USA TODAY / High schoolers ... earned their lowest marks on reading and math in two decades ... continuing a decade-long decline in student performance nationally.
description: A designed vertical graphic from the Department of Labor combining a photo background with a news-quote card. The background is a dimly lit, desaturated photograph of an empty classroom (folding tables/desks, bulletin boards) with a U.S. flag draped at the upper right. Centered over it in white serif type is the headline 'A Failing BUREAUCRACY' ('A Failing' in italic, 'BUREAUCRACY' in bold caps). Below sits a rounded white text card carrying the 'USA TODAY' logo (blue circle) and an excerpt: 'High schoolers in the United States last year earned their lowest marks on reading and math in two decades, according to "The Nation's Report Card," released on Sept. 9. The test, also known as the National Assessment of Educational Progress, is the largest nationally representative assessment of K-12 student achievement. The latest results show high school students across public and private schools backsliding in math, science and reading in 2024, continuing a decade-long decline in student performance nationally.' Visual style: somber palette, patriotic flag accent, elegant serif headline over a quoted news clipping. Propaganda technique: pairing a bleak classroom image and 'failing bureaucracy' framing with a citation from a mainstream outlet to argue the Department of Education has failed and to justify the administration's move to streamline/dismantle it. Target audience: parents, voters, and education-policy critics.

notable_text: Self-deport NOW. / LEAVE ON YOUR OWN TERMS. FREE FLIGHT HOME. $2600 EXIT BONUS. DHS.GOV/CBPHOME / FREE FLIGHT
description: A slickly designed 'self-deport' promotional graphic styled to look like an airport waiting area. The clean, brightly lit scene shows a row of black-cushioned chrome airport gate seats against a white wall and gray floor; a red hard-shell rolling suitcase with an extended handle stands in front of the seats, bearing a luggage tag printed with CBP Home / DHS branding and 'FREE FLIGHT.' Mounted on the wall is a black mechanical split-flap (Solari) departure-board sign whose white flip-letters spell: 'LEAVE ON YOUR OWN TERMS. FREE FLIGHT HOME. $2600 EXIT BONUS. DHS.GOV/CBPHOME.' Above the board, large bold text reads 'Self-deport' (blue) 'NOW.' (red). Visual style: photorealistic 3D/composite render, minimalist airport aesthetic, patriotic blue/red accents, mimicking travel-advertising design. Propaganda/persuasion technique: reframing deportation as a voluntary, dignified, even rewarded ('exit bonus,' free flight) travel choice via the CBP Home app, using familiar airport iconography to normalize it. Target audience: undocumented immigrants (and the broader public), encouraging voluntary departure.

notable_text: THE BIDEN JOB MARKET / Change in Employment Level August 2023-2024 / NATIVE-BORN WORKERS: -1,319,000 / FOREIGN-BORN WORKERS: +1,240,000
description: A designed statistics/infographic card with a clean white background and navy serif typography, headlined 'THE BIDEN JOB MARKET' (underlined). A subhead in italic reads 'Change in Employment Level August 2023-2024.' The centerpiece is an embedded FRED (Federal Reserve Economic Data) bar chart titled with a legend distinguishing 'Employment Level - Native Born' (light blue bars) from 'Employment Level - Foreign Born' (gray bars), plotted monthly from Aug 2023 to Aug 2024; the native-born bars are mostly negative (dipping to about -2,000 thousand around Dec 2023) while foreign-born bars are mostly positive, with footnotes 'Source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics via FRED' and 'fred.stlouisfed.org.' Below the chart, two large contrasting figures drive the message home: 'NATIVE-BORN WORKERS: -1,319,000' and 'FOREIGN-BORN WORKERS: +1,240,000,' with the numbers in bold navy. Footer credits: 'Sources: BLS, Federal Reserve Bank, Fox News.' Visual style: authoritative, data-driven, formal serif design. Propaganda technique: selecting a specific 12-month window and juxtaposing native-born job losses against foreign-born gains to argue the Biden administration prioritized foreign workers over Americans, contrasted (per the tweet) with Trump putting 'AMERICANS FIRST.' Target audience: American workers and voters.

notable_text: Americans' wages are up 3.8% over the year. Since President Trump took office, workers' real wages are up $700 and are on track for a $1,000 increase after his first year - meaning Americans are on track to make up one-third of the $3,000 in wages lost under Biden.
description: A plain text-only graphic, white background with dark navy-charcoal sans-serif text occupying the full frame. The first line is bold ('Americans' wages are up 3.8% over the year.') followed by lighter-weight body text reading: 'Since President Trump took office, workers' real wages are up $700 and are on track for a $1,000 increase after his first year - meaning Americans are on track to make up one-third of the $3,000 in wages lost under Biden.' There is no photo, seal, or illustration - it is a styled pull-quote/stat card resembling a screenshot of clean editorial typography. The messaging technique is a favorable economic-statistics claim that credits Trump for wage gains and blames Biden for prior wage losses, framing the administration as pro-worker. Target audience: American workers and Trump supporters on social media. The tweet text amplifies the Biden-blame angle, asserting 'Unrestrained migration under Biden CRUSHED wages for American Workers' and tying immigration restriction to wage recovery.

notable_text: MEXICO. CHILD PREDATOR. ICE in Yakima Washington.
description: A designed 'rogues-gallery' style detainee card in portrait format on a near-black background subtly textured with dark topographic contour lines. At top-left, in white bold caps preceded by a red double-chevron '>>', is the country-of-origin tag 'MEXICO'. Centered is a framed booking/mugshot photograph of a middle-aged man with slicked-back graying hair pulled into a small ponytail, a graying mustache and stubble, deep facial lines, and a flat expression, wearing a gray sweatshirt with a black collar, shot against a plain light wall. Overlaid across the bottom of the photo is a bold red banner with a folded-corner/ribbon effect bearing white uppercase text 'CHILD PREDATOR'. At the bottom-center is the circular DHS/ICE seal next to the 'ICE' wordmark, the italic word 'in', and the location 'YAKIMA WASHINGTON'. The accompanying tweet identifies the man as Antonio Castaneda-Oseguera and describes a strong-arm rape and child-molestation conviction. Messaging technique: this is a classic ICE social-media format that pairs an arrestee's mugshot with a large red crime-label and a nationality flag-tag, designed to brand immigrants as dangerous criminals ('child predator'), maximize shareability/outrage, and justify enforcement; it amassed ~951 retweets. Target audience is the general public and political base. Intent is fear/threat-based propaganda highlighting alleged criminality of an illegal alien.

notable_text: HONORING THEIR LEADERSHIP. REMEMBERING THEIR LEGACY.
description: A designed President's Day holiday graphic in dramatic black-and-white. The image is a high-contrast photo of Mount Rushmore showing the carved heads of George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, Theodore Roosevelt, and Abraham Lincoln. A faint translucent American-flag star pattern is overlaid in the upper sky for texture, and the photo has a subtle aged/grainy film treatment. Across the bottom in large white serif (Trajan-style) capitals: 'HONORING THEIR LEADERSHIP. REMEMBERING THEIR LEGACY.' Centered beneath the text is a small white Department of Labor seal. The composition is solemn and patriotic, using monument imagery to evoke reverence for past presidents. The tweet ties it to the moment: 'This President's Day, we honor the leadership and legacy of our Presidents as we approach America's 250th birthday... a vision the Trump Administration continues to uphold.' The propaganda technique links the current administration to the legacy of celebrated founders/presidents. Target audience: patriotic general public.

notable_text: Total Native-Born Employment Level; Biden Administration vs Trump Administration (so far); Inauguration; THE WHITE HOUSE
description: A White House-branded line-chart infographic on a white background titled in navy serif type 'Total Native-Born Employment Level'. A legend shows two colored series: a navy-blue line labeled 'Biden Administration' and a red line labeled 'Trump Administration (so far)'. The y-axis is labeled 'Total employment (thousands)' running from 129,000 to 134,000; the x-axis runs from Nov. 2023 to Nov. 2025. A dashed vertical line marks 'Inauguration' (early 2025). The blue (Biden) segment dips and stays roughly flat around 130,000-131,000, while the red (Trump) segment rises sharply to about 133,000+. A dark navy footer bar reads 'THE WHITE HOUSE / WASHINGTON'. The chart is designed to visually dramatize a claim that native-born employment surged under Trump. The tweet asserts 'Under President Trump, native-born Americans have accounted for ALL NET JOB GAINS. PROMISES MADE, PROMISES KEPT!' The propaganda technique is selective data framing tied to an immigration/jobs narrative favoring native-born workers. Target audience: economically-focused supporters.

notable_text: Trump's Ivy League Deals Set to Funnel Cash to Trade Schools
description: A styled screenshot of a Bloomberg news article, cropped to show the article header on a white background. Top-left small caption reads 'Politics | US'. The bold black serif headline reads 'Trump's Ivy League Deals Set to Funnel Cash to Trade Schools'. Below the headline is a row of circular social-share icons (Facebook, X, LinkedIn, email, link, gift, and a 'B' Bloomberg icon). Beneath that is a photograph of a stone archway on an Ivy League-style campus, with red-brick buildings on either side, a carved eagle/heraldic crest above the arch, wet brick walkway, two orange e-scooters parked at the base, and a lone figure walking through the arch under an overcast sky. A circular expand/fullscreen icon sits in the upper right of the photo. This is a curated press-clipping graphic used by the Department of Labor to showcase favorable coverage of the administration redirecting funds from elite universities to vocational/trade education. The propaganda technique is third-party validation via a respected outlet, supporting the 'Make America Skilled Again' theme. The terse tweet text is just emoji ('eyes' and 'fire'). Target audience: trade-school advocates and Trump supporters.

notable_text: Heroic ICE Agents Save Four-Year-Old Boy From Drowning In Minnesota Pool; 'Without the quick response and professional actions [of the officers] the outcome of this event would have likely been tragic.'
description: A designed graphic showing a screenshot of a Daily Wire+ article composited inside a realistic 3D rendering of a black smartphone (iPhone-style with notch/Dynamic Island) against a neutral gray gradient background. On the phone screen: the 'DAILY WIRE+' logo with a red 'SIGN UP' button and hamburger menu; a red 'EXCLUSIVE' tag; the headline 'Heroic ICE Agents Save Four-Year-Old Boy From Drowning In Minnesota Pool'; a bold pull-quote ''Without the quick response and professional actions [of the officers] the outcome of this event would have likely been tragic.''; byline 'By Jennie Taer'; 'Feb 25, 2026 DailyWire.com'; social-share icons and '128 Comments'; and the top of a photo showing a hotel pool with green umbrellas and lounge chairs. This is a positive-publicity card humanizing ICE agents as life-saving heroes, an image-rehabilitation messaging technique amid controversy over the agency's enforcement role. The tweet headline 'AMERICAN HEROES' and narrative about off-duty agents performing CPR reinforce the heroic framing. Target audience: general public and ICE supporters.

notable_text: Workforce Pell grants aim to support shorter-term jobs training and boost lower-income workers; The One Big Beautiful Bill Act includes a provision to fund short-term jobs training for low-income students
description: A simple text-only designed graphic styled as a cropped news article headline on a plain white background, using a heavy black sans-serif font. The large bold headline reads 'Workforce Pell grants aim to support shorter-term jobs training and boost lower-income workers'. Beneath it in smaller (but still bold) type is the subheadline/standfirst: 'The One Big Beautiful Bill Act includes a provision to fund short-term jobs training for low-income students'. There are no photographs, people, agency seals or logos visible in this crop; the design is minimalist, relying entirely on large typographic emphasis to amplify a favorable third-party news framing. The propaganda technique is the classic 'borrowed credibility' move of reposting a press headline that validates the administration's policy (the One Big Beautiful Bill / Workforce Pell), here paired with the tweet caption 'The One Big, Beautiful Bill will MAKE AMERICA SKILLED AGAIN!'. Target audience: workers, voters, and trade-school supporters, signaling tangible benefits from the legislation.

notable_text: U.S. Department of Education moves many programs to other agencies ‘to break up federal bureaucracy’; EdNC
description: A vertical designed graphic combining a news-outlet headline header with a photograph. The top portion is a white band carrying the maroon 'EdNC' (EducationNC) logo and a bold black serif headline: 'U.S. Department of Education moves many programs to other agencies ‘to break up federal bureaucracy’'. The lower two-thirds is a color photograph of Donald Trump, in a dark navy suit, white shirt and red striped tie, standing and clapping/gesturing inside an industrial facility (overhead crane, large rolled steel coils on a yellow rack at right marked '07'). Behind him a crowd of supporters in orange high-visibility jackets and hard hats cheer and photograph him; a blue banner at right reads 'AMERI...' (America). A faint circular agency watermark sits at lower left. The composition uses the borrowed-credibility technique of pairing a real third-party news headline with a triumphant rally image of the President among industrial workers, dramatizing the dismantling of the Department of Education and transfer of programs to other agencies. Caption framing criticizes 'failed bureaucracy' and declining test scores. Target audience: voters and workers supportive of shrinking federal government and boosting workforce development.

notable_text: Meet a 23-year-old electrician who was a 'good student' but skipped college to join Gen Z's blue-collar revolution. He makes 6 figures
description: A styled screenshot of a Fortune news article on a white background. The bold black serif headline reads 'Meet a 23-year-old electrician who was a 'good student' but skipped college to join Gen Z's blue-collar revolution. He makes 6 figures'. Below: byline 'BY NICK LICHTENBERG' and a date 'September 14, 2025 at 9:00 AM EDT', with a circular share icon at right. The lower half is a photograph of a smiling young man (long brown hair) in a black work shirt, arms crossed, wearing a black-and-white 'PMR' ball cap and standing in front of a red service truck/cabinet emblazoned with the 'PMR' logo and a partial phone number; shelving with parts is visible at left. This is a curated press clipping promoting the skilled-trades-over-college narrative central to DOL's 'Make America Skilled Again' campaign. The propaganda technique is third-party media validation plus an aspirational success story aimed at young people. The tweet reinforces it: 'Jacob Palmer decided to go straight into the Skilled Trades after high school instead of attending college. He now makes SIX FIGURES.' Target audience: Gen Z / young workers and parents.

notable_text: Have a question about your rights at work? We can help. 1-866-487-2365 dol.gov
description: A vertical designed public-service/promotional graphic. The background is a color photograph of an active construction/excavation site—a long dug trench in brown earth, with a yellow excavator arm and bucket prominent at the left edge and blurred workers in high-visibility vests and equipment in the soft-focus background. A semi-transparent dark blue/purple gradient overlay darkens the image for text legibility. Centered is the large white line-art seal of the U.S. Department of Labor ('Department of Labor / United States of America' with the eagle, anvil and shield). Below, in white italic serif type: 'Have a question about your rights at work? We can help.' followed by a large phone number '1-866-487-2365' and, at lower right, 'dol.gov'.
